species: mixed human fecal microbiota (chemostat model of distal colon; not a single isolate/strain)

growth_records.strain: N/A (whole fecal community from 5 human donors, #1,#2,#8,#9,#10)
growth_records.medium: sterile growth medium in chemostat vessel (composition not detailed in main text; designed to mimic human distal colon per cited ref [30])
growth_records.cite: Santiago-Rodriguez et al. Chemostat culture systems support diverse bacteriophage communities from human feces. Microbiome (2015). DOI:10.1186/s40168-015-0124-3. [PMC4638026]
growth_records.snippet: adding 100 mL into 300 mL of sterile growth medium in each vessel. The pH within each vessel was then adjusted to 6.9–7.0...maintained at 37 °C...the retention rate set to 16.67 mL/h with...sparging of O2 free N2 gas to
growth_records.notes: Community-level chemostat, not a single defined species/strain (this is the main reason confidence is low for GEM-constraint purposes). Working volume = 100 mL inoculum + 300 mL medium = 400 mL; flow (retention) rate = 16.67 mL/h. Dilution rate D = Q/V = 16.67/400 = 0.0417 h^-1 (calculated by curator from these two explicit numbers). At chemostat steady state, mu = D = 0.0417 h^-1; doubling_time = ln2/0.0417 = 16.6 h (calculated). pH range 6.9-7.0 reported as adjusted set point (midpoint 6.95 used here); anaerobic atmosphere (90% N2/5% CO2/5% H2) confirmed elsewhere in Methods.
